rationale: The citing paper explicitly says it “extends prior work on self-focused attention in marketing,” and the cited paper is a direct prior study on private self-awareness effects in consumer choice (variety seeking, compromise, preference fluency). The constructs and manipulations strongly overlap (e.g., private self-focus/self-awareness via mirror and story-writing tasks, similar consumer-choice contexts like apartments), indicating this is likely a substantive theoretical precursor rather than an incidental mention.
